General Motors

We're Hiring

Austin Tech Ridge Innovation Center

Automotive Technology AI & Software Electric Vehicles Manufacturing

General Motors has been building vehicles for over 100 years and now incorporates hundreds of millions of lines of code into its new vehicles. GM's Austin presence in Tech Ridge focuses on leveraging AI to optimize manufacturing, charging infrastructure, and EV deployment while pushing the boundaries of automotive software engineering.

Company Overview

General Motors is one of the world's largest automakers with a history spanning over a century. What began as a traditional vehicle manufacturer has evolved into a technology powerhouse, with modern GM vehicles containing hundreds of millions of lines of software code. Today, GM is at the forefront of the automotive industry's transformation, investing heavily in electric vehicles, autonomous driving, and AI-powered manufacturing.

The company's Austin facility in Tech Ridge represents GM's commitment to innovation and software engineering excellence. This location serves as a hub for developing cutting-edge technologies that power GM's next-generation vehicles and manufacturing processes.

🚗 From Vehicles to Software Platforms

Modern GM vehicles are sophisticated software platforms on wheels. With features like Super Cruise (hands-free driving), Ultifi (vehicle software platform), and OnStar connectivity, GM has become as much a software company as a vehicle manufacturer. The Austin team plays a crucial role in developing and maintaining these systems.

Austin Tech Ridge Operations

GM's Austin presence focuses on software development, AI research, and technology innovation. The Tech Ridge location attracts top engineering talent from the University of Texas and Austin's thriving tech ecosystem.

🤖

AI & Machine Learning

Developing AI systems to optimize manufacturing efficiency, improve vehicle quality control, and enhance autonomous driving capabilities.

  • • Computer vision for quality inspection
  • • Predictive maintenance algorithms
  • • Manufacturing process optimization
  • • Supply chain intelligence
⚡

EV Infrastructure

Building software systems to support electric vehicle charging networks, battery management, and energy optimization.

  • • Charging station location planning
  • • Battery health monitoring
  • • Energy grid integration
  • • Route optimization for EVs
💻

Vehicle Software

Creating the software that powers in-vehicle experiences, connectivity features, and advanced driver assistance systems.

  • • Infotainment systems
  • • Over-the-air (OTA) updates
  • • Connectivity platforms
  • • Driver assistance features
🏭

Manufacturing Tech

Leveraging technology to make GM's manufacturing processes more efficient, flexible, and sustainable.

  • • Factory automation systems
  • • Robotics coordination
  • • Production line optimization
  • • Digital twin simulations

The Software-Defined Vehicle

GM is transforming into a software-centric company, with vehicles becoming platforms for continuous innovation and improvement throughout their lifecycle.

📊 By The Numbers

Ultium

Key Software Platforms

🎯 Ultifi Platform

GM's end-to-end software platform that enables continuous updates, new feature deployment, and third-party integrations. Ultifi allows GM to add capabilities to vehicles after purchase through over-the-air updates.

🚘 Super Cruise

GM's hands-free driver assistance technology that uses AI, computer vision, and precise mapping to enable hands-free driving on compatible highways.

📱 OnStar & Connectivity

Connected vehicle services providing safety features, diagnostics, navigation, and vehicle management through mobile apps and in-vehicle systems.

Careers at GM Austin

🚀 Open Positions: 391+ Roles

GM is hiring extensively across software engineering, data science, AI/ML, and product development. The Austin location offers opportunities to work on cutting-edge automotive technology at a company with the scale and resources to bring innovations to millions of customers.

Software Engineering Opportunities

Embedded Systems Engineers

Develop software that runs directly on vehicle hardware, controlling everything from powertrains to safety systems. Work with real-time operating systems and safety-critical code.

Cloud & Backend Engineers

Build scalable cloud infrastructure supporting millions of connected vehicles. Develop APIs, microservices, and data pipelines for vehicle telemetry and services.

AI/ML Engineers

Apply machine learning to autonomous driving, manufacturing optimization, predictive maintenance, and customer experience enhancement.

Mobile & Frontend Developers

Create mobile apps and in-vehicle user interfaces that provide intuitive experiences for GM customers across iOS, Android, and embedded platforms.

Why Join GM Austin?

  • ✓ Work on technology that impacts millions of vehicles
  • ✓ Shape the future of transportation and mobility
  • ✓ Fortune 500 stability with startup-like innovation
  • ✓ Comprehensive benefits and competitive compensation
  • ✓ Austin's vibrant tech community and lifestyle
  • ✓ Career growth in a transforming industry

Electric Vehicle Leadership

GM has committed to an all-electric future, with plans to eliminate tailpipe emissions from new light-duty vehicles by 2035. The company's Ultium battery platform provides the foundation for a diverse portfolio of EVs across all brands and price points.

🔋

Ultium Platform

Flexible battery architecture supporting ranges from 300-450+ miles

⚡

Fast Charging

Up to 100 miles of range in 10 minutes with DC fast charging

🌎

Zero Emissions Goal

Carbon neutral by 2040 across global operations

Shape the Future of Mobility

Join GM's Austin team and work on automotive technology that matters
